evals.prompt: who should review this pr? i changed a bunch of files in src/auth/ and src/api/middleware/
evals.expected_output: Detects the PR, gets changed files, filters out generated files, gathers candidates via git log history and CODEOWNERS. Scores candidates using line-weighted ownership, commit count, CODEOWNERS match, and directory familiarity. Applies penalties for recent reviewer overlap and high review load. Presents up to 3 candidates with score breakdowns and concrete expertise reasons. Asks before adding reviewers.
expectations: Excludes generated files (*.generated.*, lock files, vendor/, etc.) from the analysis
Presents score breakdown with concrete numbers and expertise reasons (e.g., 'Owns 60% of changed lines in src/auth/')
Applies penalties for recent reviewer overlap — does NOT treat 'reviewed your recent PRs' as a positive signal

evals.prompt: /pr-reviewers --auto, the PR only adds 2 new files in a brand new directory src/notifications/
evals.expected_output: Recognizes all files are new (no git blame history). Falls back to directory familiarity — finds experts on parent directory (src/) via git log. Scores using directory familiarity weight. In --auto mode, adds recommended reviewers directly without asking. Shows score breakdowns.
expectations: Handles new files correctly — uses parent directory git log for candidate gathering since new files have no blame history
Adds reviewers directly without asking in --auto mode
Still presents score breakdowns even in --auto mode so the user understands the choices

evals.prompt: find reviewers for my pr. heads up — alice has reviewed my last 4 prs and bob is already assigned to like 8 open reviews
evals.expected_output: Gathers candidates normally, then applies penalties: alice gets a 0.7x multiplier for reviewing 3+ of last 5 PRs, bob gets a 0.6x multiplier for 8+ open review requests. These penalties may push them down the ranking even if they have high base scores. Results show the penalty clearly (e.g., 'currently reviewing 8 PRs').
expectations: Applies the 0.7x penalty to alice for reviewing 3+ of the last 5 author PRs
Applies the 0.6x penalty to bob for 8+ open review requests
Shows penalty attribution in results (e.g., 'currently reviewing 8 PRs') so user understands the ranking
